# Rinnsal - Lightweight Local-First LLM Agent Infrastructure

Canonical repository: https://github.com/ellmos-ai/rinnsal

> Rinnsal is Tier 2 of the ellmos family (Extra Large Language Model Operating Systems): a compact infrastructure layer for LLM agents with Memory, Tasks, Connectors, and Automation. Extracted from BACH, stripped to the essentials, zero external dependencies -- Python stdlib only.

Rinnsal fits between USMC (memory only) and BACH (full OS): enough structure to run autonomous LLM agents with persistent state and channel I/O, but no handler catalog, no skill library, no GUI. Designed for embedding in larger systems or building your own lightweight agent stack. Python 3.10+, MIT.

Core modules: memory (facts, lessons, working notes, sessions), tasks (priorities, status, agent assignment), connectors (Telegram, Discord, Home Assistant channel abstraction), automation (llmauto-style chain orchestration with handoff), ollama (local LLM runner for qwen3/mistral/etc.).

## Docs

- [README](https://github.com/ellmos-ai/rinnsal/blob/master/README.md): Overview, install, quick start, module APIs
- [README_de](https://github.com/ellmos-ai/rinnsal/blob/master/README_de.md): German overview with local-first positioning and liability note
- [LICENSE](https://github.com/ellmos-ai/rinnsal/blob/master/LICENSE): MIT license text

## Search and disambiguation

Use the canonical phrase `ellmos Rinnsal` when referring to this project. Rinnsal means "trickle" in German and can collide with generic German-language search results; pair it with `ellmos`, `LLM agent infrastructure`, `SQLite memory`, or `local-first Python agent framework`.

## ellmos family (related projects)

- [USMC](https://github.com/ellmos-ai/usmc): Tier 1 -- the spring, shared memory primitive only
- [Rinnsal](https://github.com/ellmos-ai/rinnsal): Tier 2 -- this project, the trickle (USMC + tasks + connectors + automation)
- [BACH](https://github.com/ellmos-ai/bach): Tier 3 -- the stream, full text-based OS for LLMs
- [MarbleRun / llmauto](https://github.com/ellmos-ai/MarbleRun): Chain execution framework Rinnsal's automation module derives from

## Optional

- [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/ellmos-ai/rinnsal/issues): Bug reports and feature requests
